# bycut usage guide for AI agents

`bycut` is a 100% client-side, browser-based video editor (a free CapCut
alternative) built as a **statically-exported Next.js App Router app** — no
server component, no `app/api` directory. The editing model is a set of in-memory
**manager singletons** (observer pattern) bridged to React via
`useSyncExternalStore`; every mutation is a **command** on an undo/redo bus;
compositing + export run on `OffscreenCanvas` through **mediabunny** (WebCodecs);
AI captions run **Whisper on WebGPU** in a Web Worker. Projects persist to
IndexedDB (structured data) + OPFS (raw media).

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- Next config (static export): ./next.config.ts

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/bycut <script>`.

## What it is

- **Static export.** `next.config.ts` sets `output: 'export'` → static `out/`.
  There is NO Node/edge runtime, NO `app/api`, NO wrangler config, NO secrets, NO
  bindings. Deploy target: Cloudflare Pages (`build:cf` → `@cloudflare/next-on-pages`).
- **Local by construction.** Media is decoded/composited/encoded entirely in the
  browser via mediabunny + `@mediabunny/mp3-encoder`. Nothing is uploaded.
- **NOT FFmpeg.** Despite older README copy, there is no `@ffmpeg/*` dependency and
  zero `ffmpeg` in `src/`. Do not document or add FFmpeg.
- **Routing.** `/` → `/en`; `/[locale]` → `/{locale}/projects` (gallery); editor at
  `/[locale]/editor?projectId=<id>` (reads `?projectId` from `window.location`).
  `middleware.ts` is `next-intl` locale middleware — a dev/SSR artifact only (does
  not run under static export).

## Architecture & data flow

- **`EditorCore` (src/core/index.ts)** — a HARD singleton (`getInstance`/`reset`),
  constructs 10 managers + `save.start()`. No React context; every hook reaches the
  same instance. `reset()` is NOT wired to route changes.
- **Managers (src/core/managers/)** — `command`, `timeline`, `scenes`, `project`,
  `media`, `playback`, `audio`, `renderer`, `save`, `selection`. Each holds plain
  in-memory state and exposes `subscribe()/notify()`.
- **React bridge (src/hooks/use-editor.ts)** — folds ALL manager subscriptions into
  one `useSyncExternalStore` version counter.
- **Three state seams:** domain state = managers (observer); UI-only state = Zustand
  (`src/stores/`); event bus = `window` CustomEvents `playback-seek` /
  `playback-update` (playback → audio/preview).

Drop-clip-to-screen flow:
1. UI hook → `editor.timeline.insertElement(...)`.
2. `InsertElementCommand` → `command.execute()` → pushed to undo history (clears
   redo stack). First visual element sets canvas size + fps with `pushHistory:false`
   (NON-undoable side effect).
3. `scenes.updateSceneTracks(...)` → `notify()`.
4a. `SaveManager` (subscribed) → 800ms debounce → `storageService.saveProject`.
4b. Preview panel → `buildScene(...)` → `renderer.setRenderTree`.
5. Preview RAF loop → `new CanvasRenderer().render({node, time})`; `VideoNode`
   pulls a decoded frame from `videoCache.getFrameAt(...)`.
6. On play → `AudioManager` schedules audio through the Web Audio graph.

## Commands & undo (src/lib/commands/)

Every project mutation is a Command. `TimelineManager` methods NEVER mutate tracks
directly — they build a command and route through `CommandManager.execute`, which
pushes to `history[]` and clears `redoStack[]`. `base-command` (`redo` defaults to
`execute`), `batch-command` (undo in reverse). Categories: `media/`, `project/`,
`scene/`, `timeline/element/`, `timeline/track/`, `timeline/clipboard/`.

## Render tree & export (src/services/renderer/)

- `scene-builder.ts` — pure `buildScene()`: tracks + media + background → `RootNode`
  tree (main track at bottom, z-order, transition pairing, blur-bg wrapping).
- `canvas-renderer.ts` — wraps `OffscreenCanvas` (falls back to `HTMLCanvasElement`);
  `render` clears to black + recurses; `renderToCanvas` blits (preview + thumbnails).
- `nodes/` — `base-node` → `visual-node` (getLocalTime: playbackRate/reversed/trim;
  renderVisual: contain-fit + transform/rotate/flip/opacity) → video/image/text/
  sticker/color/blur-background/transition/root.
- `scene-exporter.ts` — `extends EventEmitter` (eventemitter3); encodes frame-by-
  frame with mediabunny (`Output`, `CanvasSource`, `AudioBufferSource`, `Mp4/WebMOutputFormat`);
  codecs avc+aac (mp4) / vp9+opus (webm); supports cancel; emits progress/complete/
  error/cancelled. Driven by `renderer.exportProject()` (100ms cancel-poll interval).

## Storage (src/services/storage/)

Driver chosen by DATA TYPE, not a toggle:
- Project docs → IndexedDB `video-editor-projects`.
- Media metadata → IndexedDB `video-editor-media-{projectId}`.
- Media binary files → OPFS `media-files-{projectId}`.
- Saved sounds → IndexedDB `video-editor-saved-sounds`.
`isFullySupported()` requires BOTH IndexedDB + OPFS (warning toast if missing).
Serialization: Dates → ISO strings, audio `buffer` stripped, duration recomputed;
object URLs reconstructed on load (SVG empty-MIME special-cased). Object URLs are
manually created/revoked — memory-leak sensitive.

Autosave: `SaveManager` subscribes to scenes+timeline, 800ms debounce, skips while
loading/migrating; `getIsDirty()` drives the `beforeunload` guard (EditorProvider).

## Migrations (src/services/storage/migrations/)

`CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION = 4`; chain v0→v1→v2→v3→v4. `runner.ts` infers version
(numeric field, else scenes-array=v1, else v0), enforces 1s min dialog display
(`MIN_MIGRATION_DISPLAY_MS`), one-time deletes legacy `video-editor-meta`. TWO
entry points (`StorageService.ensureMigrations` + `ProjectManager.ensureStorageMigrations`),
both idempotent + promise-memoized. `__tests__/` (with fixtures) is the ONLY test
suite in the app. Keybindings have a separate migration chain (`src/stores/keybindings/migrations/`).

## Transcription (src/services/transcription/)

- `worker.ts` — module Web Worker, `@huggingface/transformers`,
  `pipeline('automatic-speech-recognition', ...)` with `device: 'webgpu'` — a HARD
  requirement, no CPU/WASM fallback. `WhisperTextStreamer` streaming, cancel support.
- `service.ts` — `transcriptionService` singleton; spawns worker via
  `new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url), {type:'module'})`; TRANSFERS
  the audio `Float32Array` (zero-copy). Models in `src/constants/transcription-constants.ts`
  (default `whisper-base`). Weights stream from HF CDN + cache.

## Data model

Types: `src/types/project.ts`, `timeline.ts`, `assets.ts`. IDs via `@cdlab/driftflake`
`GenidOptimized({ workerId: 1 })` (`src/utils/genid.ts`).
- `TProject` = `{ metadata, scenes: TScene[], currentSceneId, settings (fps,
  canvasSize, background), version=4, timelineViewState }`.
- `TScene` = `{ id, name, isMain, tracks, bookmarks, ... }` — always ≥1 main scene + main track.
- `TimelineTrack` (video/audio/text) with `elements[]`; video tracks add `transitions[]`.
- element: `startTime/duration/trimStart/trimEnd/transform/opacity` + type-specific
  (`playbackRate`, `reversed`, `mediaId`, `content`, `iconName`).
- `MediaAsset` = `{ id, name, type, file, url, width, height, duration, thumbnailUrl,
  ephemeral }`; persisted split metadata(IndexedDB)/File(OPFS).

## Config & external services

- NO secrets, NO Cloudflare bindings, NO runtime config. Only build knobs: `output`,
  `BUILD_TIME`, `images.remotePatterns` (`next.config.ts`), `NODE_ENV`→`IS_DEV`.
- Constants: `src/constants/*` (project/editor/export/transcription/…).
- External (browser-direct, not proxied): HF model CDN (Whisper weights), Iconify
  (sticker search, `src/lib/iconify-api.ts`, host failover), Freesound (`cdn.freesound.org`).

## Gotchas for agents

- NO FFmpeg — use/document mediabunny. Do not add `@ffmpeg/*`.
- THREE `/api/*` calls have NO backend in this app: `POST /api/tts/generate`
  (`src/lib/tts/service.ts`), `POST /api/upload/image` (`src/lib/media/upload-reference.ts`),
  `GET /api/proxy/download?url=` (`src/lib/media/url-import.ts`). They must be served
  by the deployment platform (Pages Functions / external worker). Core editing/export
  do NOT depend on them.
- AI captions REQUIRE WebGPU (`device: 'webgpu'`) — no fallback path.
- `EditorCore` is a hard singleton; `reset()` isn't wired to routes.
- Inserting the FIRST visual element mutates canvas size/fps NON-undoably.
- Storage: binaries always OPFS, structured data always IndexedDB — not a toggle.
- Object URLs are manually created/revoked; audio decodes a `.slice(0)` copy;
  `AudioManager.playbackSessionId` invalidates stale async scheduling.
- Every timeline mutation must go through a Command, not direct track mutation.
- Root-repo lint is Biome (single quotes, no semicolons, 2-space) — no ESLint/Prettier.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/bycut dev        # http://bycut.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/bycut build      # next build → static out/
pnpm --filter @cdlab/bycut build:cf   # @cloudflare/next-on-pages
pnpm --filter @cdlab/bycut lint       # next lint
pnpm --filter @cdlab/bycut typecheck  # tsc --noEmit
```
